ksa_text: Knowledge of AI ethics principles and fairness evaluation methods, including bias detection and measurement techniques, fairness metrics, representativeness assessment, and the relationship between training data characteristics and model equity outcomes.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: knowledge maps directly to Model Governance Analyst scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("bias detect", "fairness.{0,10} ?:assess|metric|test|audit", "ai ethics"). STRM composite is moderate (0.3947); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation present: 1 match(es) in RSK job postings or certification scope. Competency appears in RSK role descriptions — sufficient to uphold.
